title: Authentication, Authorization, RBAC, Service Accounts, Tokens, and Impersonation · Cluster Case: K8s Role Binding Scope
summary: A CI workload uses the default service account and a long-lived token while a broad ClusterRoleBinding permits secret reads and workload creation across tenants. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s role binding scope increment in least-privilege identity and authorization policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.
Separate external human users and groups, ServiceAccounts, node identities, workload identity federation, image pull identity, and bootstrap credentials.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Role Binding Scope · Theory K8s Role Binding Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Competency probe: Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example workload uses the default ServiceAccount, automounts a broad token, and receives wildcard cluster-admin through a group binding. Derive subject, verb, resource, namespace, token audience, impersonation, and least-privilege proof. Competency probe: Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects. A defensible solution applies K8s Role Binding Scope, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to least-privilege identity and authorization policy.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-identity-rbac-serviceaccounts-5
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-identity-rbac-serviceaccounts-5
    artifact: rbac-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s identity rbac serviceaccounts 5
    environment: managed canary cluster with provider-specific load balancing
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked5
    change: |
      serviceAccount: intake/status-api
      automountServiceAccountToken: false
      token: projected-audience-intake-api
      role: get-configmap-status-only
      binding: namespace-intake
      review: auth-can-i-and-escalation-map
    verify: kubectl auth can-i --list --as system:serviceaccount:intake:status-api -n intake
    failure: Reject broad ClusterRoleBinding, long-lived static tokens, wildcard verbs, secret reads, bind or escalate paths, or workload creation as harmless.
    repair: Separate human and workload identity, scope verbs and resources, use bound audience tokens, test authorization, and review escalation periodically.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A RoleBinding can grant a Role access to resources in every namespace. This fails the K8s Role Binding Scope contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

content.value: Kubernetes Security, RBAC, Pod Security, and Multi-Tenancy (Kubernetes 1.36 security guidance, current 2026-07-14) — Controls identities, authentication, authorization, RBAC, service accounts, bound tokens, Pod Security Standards and Admission, security contexts, Secrets, admission, isolation, and audit.

content.label: Mastery evidence
content.value: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Construct namespace-scoped Roles and RoleBindings or justified ClusterRoles and ClusterRoleBindings from verbs, resources, names, non-resource URLs, and subjects.
